note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the deposition and patterning of materials on a substrate using advanced techniques such as ALD (Atomic Layer Deposition) and etching. The goal is to study the effects of different thicknesses of a gold cap layer on the surface morphology of the substrate.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person in a cleanroom environment is shown handling a substrate, preparing it for deposition.",
    "The substrate is then placed into an ALD chamber where a thin film of material is deposited.",
    "After deposition, the substrate is transferred to an etching chamber where it is treated at 80°C.",
    "The process is repeated with varying thicknesses of a gold cap layer applied before etching."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Substrate: A silicon wafer coated with a 100 nm layer of SiO2.",
    "Materials: Gold, ALD precursor, etchant."
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "ALD chamber equipped with a gauge controller.",
    "Etching chamber with heating elements.",
    "Microscope for observing surface morphology.",
    "Computer for controlling the ALD process."
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "The thickness of the gold cap layers varies between 5 nm and 20 nm.",
    "The substrate is observed under a microscope at 100x magnification.",
    "The temperature during etching is set at 80°C."
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "The substrate is moved from the ALD chamber to the etching chamber.",
    "Different thicknesses of the gold cap layer are applied before etching."
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Surface morphology images of the substrate after etching with varying gold cap thicknesses.",
    "Quantitative data on particle size distribution and density."
  ],
